problem:
If $\begin{vmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{vmatrix} = 5,$ then find
\[\begin{vmatrix} a - c & b - d \\ c & d \end{vmatrix}.\]

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the first step in finding the determinant of the matrix $\begin{vmatrix} a - c & b - d \\ c & d \end{vmatrix}$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of the determinant $\begin{vmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{vmatrix}$ being equal to 5 in relation to the given matrix?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: How does the determinant change when we subtract $c$ from $a$ and $d$ from $b$ in the given matrix?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the equation $(a - c)d - (b - d)c$ is equal to the determinant of the given matrix?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the geometric interpretation of the determinant of a matrix?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: In general, what properties or transformations affect the determinant of a matrix?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other examples where a similar thought process and steps can be used to determine the determinant of a matrix?
classified_questions.label: e
